content: We can reframe the ICM curiosity mechanism for autonomous agents to use case-based reasoning as its core novelty detector: instead of measuring prediction error to quantify novelty, an ICM-powered agent treats every unvisited state as a new problem to match against its stored case library of previously explored states, receiving intrinsic reward proportional to how poorly the new state matches any existing case. This gives autonomous agents a more human-aligned curiosity drive that naturally prioritizes high-impact, truly uncharted exploration over merely unpredictable random noise.
